ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for Soan Papdi; mapped to the closest parent confectionery food product class (FOODON_03301710) as the most defensible FoodOn anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Soan Papdi; A0CKL (Sugar confectionery) is the closest parent FoodEx2 category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Indian confectionery item.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Soan Papdi is approximately 30 g, equivalent to one or two small pieces or a modest portion from a box.

nutrient_source.title: Sohn Papdi — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo entry for Sohn Papdi (cr_id F13010007), confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 14 g/100 g based on the known ghee/vanaspati fat composition of traditional Soan Papdi; this sub-fraction is not separately reported in the HPB record and is documented as an estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sugar Confectionery and Wheat-Flour-Based Sweets — analogous estimate based on refined wheat flour and high-sugar confectionery entries
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Soan Papdi exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 75 (high) based on the closest analogous entries: refined wheat-flour and chickpea-flour confectionery with high sugar content (>40 g/100 g). Glycemic load per 30 g serving = (75 × 65 × 0.30) / 100 = 14.6 g. This is a conservative analogous estimate; actual GI may vary with preparation method and fat content.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sugar (46.4 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (1.4 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; high saturated fat (~14 g/100 g) contributes to arterial stiffness.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Soan Papdi due to its very high sugar and saturated fat content, which promote insulin-mediated sodium retention and vascular stiffness; even a 30 g portion delivers approximately 14 g of sugar and 4 g of saturated fat.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 46.4 g/100 g far exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling; combined with 65 g refined carbohydrate/100 g and only 1.4 g dietary fibre, this food causes rapid and sustained postprandial glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Soan Papdi is contraindicated for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; its extremely high sugar and refined carbohydrate content with negligible fibre produces a high glycaemic response and should be avoided entirely, with no safe portion size identified.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~14 g/100 g and trans fat ~0.5 g/100 g from ghee or vanaspati (partially hydrogenated vegetable fat) raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis; high sugar further elevates triglycerides.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Soan Papdi due to its high saturated fat, estimated trans fat from vanaspati, and very high sugar content, all of which independently elevate cardiovascular risk through LDL elevation, triglyceride increase, and endothelial dysfunction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated trans fat (~0.5 g/100 g from vanaspati), saturated fat (~14 g/100 g), and refined sugar (46.4 g/100 g) all independently trigger pro-inflammatory pathways relevant to RA.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Soan Papdi; its trans fat from vanaspati, high saturated fat, and refined sugar content are strongly pro-inflammatory and may exacerbate joint inflammation and disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Soan Papdi (also spelled Sohn Papdi, Sohan Papdi, or Soan Papri) is a traditional North Indian confectionery made primarily from wheat flour, chickpea flour (besan), ghee or vanaspati (partially hydrogenated vegetable fat), sugar, and cardamom, garnished with almonds and melon seeds (magaz). The sweet is prepared by cooking a sugar syrup to the soft-ball stage, then pulling and aerating the mixture repeatedly to create its characteristic flaky, cotton-candy-like texture that dissolves on the tongue. It is pale golden in colour, extremely light and crisp, with a sweet, mildly nutty flavour and a delicate cardamom aroma. Soan Papdi is widely consumed across India and the Indian diaspora in Singapore and Southeast Asia, particularly during festivals such as Diwali and Holi, and is commonly gifted in decorative boxes. Nutritionally, it is a high-energy (530 kcal/100 g), high-sugar (46.4 g/100 g), high-fat (28.4 g/100 g) confectionery with low dietary fibre (1.4 g/100 g) and very low sodium (3 mg/100 g). Its high refined carbohydrate and sugar load makes it a high-glycaemic food (estimated GI ~75). It is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its sugar, saturated fat, and potential trans fat content from vanaspati.
common_user_queries: How many calories in soan papdi?
What is sohn papdi made of?
Is soan papdi suitable for hypertension?
What is the glycemic index of soan papdi?
What are the ingredients in sohan papdi?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Sohn Papdi (cr_id F13010007) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated from known composition of ghee/vanaspati-based Indian confectionery and documented as estimates. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as no direct measurement exists.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this traditional Indian confectionery preparation are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F13010007) as of 2026; these fields were also confirmed absent from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for comparable Indian confectionery. saturated_fat_g (14.0 g/100 g) and trans_fat_g (0.5 g/100 g) are estimates based on the known ghee/vanaspati fat composition of traditional Soan Papdi preparations; these values are not directly reported in the HPB source. The input record used the condition key 'diabetes' which has been updated to 'type2_diabetes' per Veritas naming rules. The food is also known in Singapore as 'Sohn papdi' per the HPB database. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Soan Papdi was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026. Ontology match types set to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 as no exact code exists for this specific Indian confectionery.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English name 'Soan Papdi' used. Hindi local name सोन पापड़ी, Tamil சோன் பப்டி, Chinese 松脆糖果/鬆脆糖果, Malay and romanized forms all present. Indian cuisine origin correctly identified.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal 530.0, protein_g 3.5, fat_g 28.4, carbohydrate_g 65.0 all present and sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id F13010007, confidence 0.95). nutrient_source fully populated.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 75 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database), gi_category 'high', glycemic_load_per_serving 14.6 for 30 g serving, gi_source fully populated with analogous estimate basis documented.

Match types corrected to 'closest_parent' per allowed values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none']. Gap notes provided for both. SNOMED CT null with gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, covers preparation method, texture, taste, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. common_user_queries and ingredient_synonyms populated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high), Type 2 Diabetes (high), Cardiovascular Disease (high). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Condition key is type2_diabetes with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes'.
